canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [1]
text_original: Tai Kang occupied the throne like a personator of the dead. By idleness and dissipation he extinguished his virtue, till the black-haired people all wavered in their allegiance. He, however, pursued his pleasure and wanderings without any self-restraint. He went out to hunt beyond the Luo, and a hundred days elapsed without his returning. (On this) Yi, the prince of Qiong, taking advantage of the discontent of the people, resisted (his return) on (the south of) the He. The (king's) five brothers had attended their mother in following him, and were waiting for him on the north of the Luo; and (when they heard of Yi's movement), all full of dissatisfaction, they related the Cautions of the great Yu in the form of songs.
text_normalized: Tai Kang occupied the throne like a personator of the dead. By idleness and dissipation he extinguished his virtue, till the black-haired people all wavered in their allegiance. He, however, pursued his pleasure and wanderings without any self-restraint. He went out to hunt beyond the Luo, and a hundred days elapsed without his returning. (On this) Yi, the prince of Qiong, taking advantage of the discontent of the people, resisted (his return) on (the south of) the He. The (king's) five brothers had attended their mother in following him, and were waiting for him on the north of the Luo; and (when they heard of Yi's movement), all full of dissatisfaction, they related the Cautions of the great Yu in the form of songs.

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [2]
text_original: The first said,
text_normalized: The first said,

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [3]
text_original: 'It was the lesson of our great ancestor:
text_normalized: 'It was the lesson of our great ancestor:

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [4]
text_original: The people should be cherished, And not looked down upon.
text_normalized: The people should be cherished, And not looked down upon.

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [5]
text_original: The people are the root of a country;
text_normalized: The people are the root of a country;

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [6]
text_original: The root firm, the country is tranquil.
text_normalized: The root firm, the country is tranquil.

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [7]
text_original: When I look at all under heaven, Of the simple men and simple women,
text_normalized: When I look at all under heaven, Of the simple men and simple women,

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [8]
text_original: Any one may surpass me.
text_normalized: Any one may surpass me.

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [9]
text_original: If the One man err repeatedly,
text_normalized: If the One man err repeatedly,

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [10]
text_original: Should dissatisfaction be waited for till it appears?
text_normalized: Should dissatisfaction be waited for till it appears?

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [11]
text_original: Before it is seen, it should be guarded against.
text_normalized: Before it is seen, it should be guarded against.

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [12]
text_original: In my dealing with the millions of the people,
text_normalized: In my dealing with the millions of the people,

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [13]
text_original: I should feel as much anxiety as if I were driving six horses with rotten reins.
text_normalized: I should feel as much anxiety as if I were driving six horses with rotten reins.

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [14]
text_original: The ruler of men -
text_normalized: The ruler of men -

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [15]
text_original: How should he be but reverent (of his duties)?'
text_normalized: How should he be but reverent (of his duties)?'

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [16]
text_original: The second said,
text_normalized: The second said,

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [17]
text_original: 'It is in the Lessons:
text_normalized: 'It is in the Lessons:

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [18]
text_original: When the palace is a wild of lust, And the country is a wild for hunting;
text_normalized: When the palace is a wild of lust, And the country is a wild for hunting;

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [19]
text_original: When spirits are liked, and music is the delight;
text_normalized: When spirits are liked, and music is the delight;

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [20]
text_original: When there are lofty roofs and carved walls;
text_normalized: When there are lofty roofs and carved walls;

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [21]
text_original: The existence of any one of these things
text_normalized: The existence of any one of these things

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [22]
text_original: Has never been but the prelude to ruin.'
text_normalized: Has never been but the prelude to ruin.'

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [23]
text_original: The third said,
text_normalized: The third said,

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [24]
text_original: 'There was the lord of Tao and Tang Who possessed this region of Ji.
text_normalized: 'There was the lord of Tao and Tang Who possessed this region of Ji.

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [25]
text_original: Now we have fallen from his ways, And thrown into confusion his rules and laws;
text_normalized: Now we have fallen from his ways, And thrown into confusion his rules and laws;

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [26]
text_original: The consequence is extinction and ruin.'
text_normalized: The consequence is extinction and ruin.'

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [27]
text_original: The fourth said,
text_normalized: The fourth said,

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [28]
text_original: 'Brightly intelligent was our ancestor,
text_normalized: 'Brightly intelligent was our ancestor,

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [29]
text_original: Sovereign of the myriad regions.
text_normalized: Sovereign of the myriad regions.

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [30]
text_original: He had canons, he had patterns, Which he transmitted to his posterity.
text_normalized: He had canons, he had patterns, Which he transmitted to his posterity.

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [31]
text_original: The standard stone and the equalizing quarter Were in the royal treasury.
text_normalized: The standard stone and the equalizing quarter Were in the royal treasury.

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [32]
text_original: Wildly have we dropt the clue he gave us,
text_normalized: Wildly have we dropt the clue he gave us,

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [33]
text_original: Overturning our temple, and extinguishing our sacrifices.'
text_normalized: Overturning our temple, and extinguishing our sacrifices.'

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [34]
text_original: The fifth said,
text_normalized: The fifth said,

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [35]
text_original: 'Oh! whither shall we turn?
text_normalized: 'Oh! whither shall we turn?

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [36]
text_original: The thoughts in my breast make me sad
text_normalized: The thoughts in my breast make me sad

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [37]
text_original: All the people are hostile to us;
text_normalized: All the people are hostile to us;

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [38]
text_original: On whom can we rely?
text_normalized: On whom can we rely?

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [39]
text_original: Anxieties crowd together in our hearts;
text_normalized: Anxieties crowd together in our hearts;

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [40]
text_original: Thick as are our faces, they are covered with blushes.
text_normalized: Thick as are our faces, they are covered with blushes.

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [41]
text_original: We have not been careful of our virtue;
text_normalized: We have not been careful of our virtue;

canonical_ref: 尚書·夏書 五子之歌 [42]
text_original: And though we repent, we cannot over-take the past.'
text_normalized: And though we repent, we cannot over-take the past.'
